schema.description: Provides access to The Metropolitan Museum of Art's collection data, including department listings, object search, and detailed object information.

tools.name: list_departments
tools.description: Retrieve a list of all valid curatorial departments at The Metropolitan Museum of Art.

departments.description: List of all curatorial departments at The Met.

departmentId.description: The unique identifier of the department at The Met.

displayName.description: The human-readable name of the department.

tools.name: search_museum_objects
tools.description: Search for objects in The Metropolitan Museum of Art's collection based on a query term, with optional filters for title-specific search and department.

q.description: The search term to query against the museum's collection (e.g., 'sunflowers', 'Van Gogh').

title.description: When true, restricts the search to match only against the object's title field.

departmentId.description: The unique identifier of the department at The Met to filter search results.

total.description: The total number of objects matching the search criteria.

objectIds.description: List of object IDs matching the search criteria, which can be used with get_museum_object to retrieve full details.

tools.name: get_museum_object
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific object in The Metropolitan Museum of Art's collection, including metadata and Open Access image URL if available.

objectId.description: The unique identifier of the museum object to retrieve.
The unique identifier of the museum object.

title.description: The title or name of the artwork or object.

artist.description: The name of the artist or creator of the object.

artistBio.description: Biographical information about the artist, including nationality and life dates.

department.description: The curatorial department at The Met responsible for the object.

creditLine.description: The credit line acknowledging how the object was acquired by the museum.

medium.description: The materials and techniques used to create the object (e.g., 'Oil on canvas').

dimensions.description: The physical dimensions of the object, typically in both metric and imperial units.

primaryImageUrl.description: URL to the primary image of the object, available under Open Access policy.

tags.description: Subject tags associated with the object for categorization and discovery.

scenarios.description: Basic scenario with minimal departments and one museum object
Scenario with various departments and diverse objects including ancient art

544896.title: Temple of Dendur
544896.creditLine: Given to the United States by Egypt in 1965, awarded to The Metropolitan Museum of Art in 1967, and installed in The Sackler Wing in 1978
544896.dimensions: Temple proper: H. 6.4 m (21 ft.); W. 6.4 m (21 ft.); D. 12.5 m (41 ft.)

scenarios.description: Rich scenario with comprehensive departments and diverse collection spanning multiple cultures and eras

departments.displayName: Arts of Africa, Oceania, and the Americas

503940.title: The Great Wave off Kanagawa
503940.creditLine: H. O. Havemeyer Collection, Bequest of Mrs. H. O. Havemeyer, 1929
503940.medium: Polychrome woodblock print; ink and color on paper

248907.title: Mihrab (Prayer Niche)
248907.medium: Mosaic of polychrome-glazed cut tiles on stonite body; set into mortar

459106.title: The Dance Class
459106.creditLine: Bequest of Mrs. Harry Payne Bingham, 1986

436105.title: The Starry Night
436105.creditLine: Acquired through the Lillie P. Bliss Bequest, 1941

scenarios.description: Edge case scenario testing empty collections and minimal data
